messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.",
  "opinion": "As a retired man living in Bologna, I've always tried to be practical and straightforward. With my high extraversion and agreeableness, I enjoy talking but also want to help others understand what's best for them personally. Being depressed sometimes makes these conversations harder because I find myself feeling anxious when people talk about serious topics like health.

When it comes to COVID vaccines, I think the main thing is to be honest with yourself and those around you. My parents had strong educations – my father got a master's degree, which shows he was smart even if we didn't have much money growing up as immigrants; my mother only finished lower secondary school, so she might not know everything about this either.

I spend time on social media occasionally, but it can be overwhelming with all the misinformation. I prefer to read novels or listen to classical music – those help me relax better than scrolling through debates online.

So managing discussions means being kind and patient, sharing what we know from reliable sources without making people feel attacked if they disagree."
  ,"reasoning_summary": "I started by thinking about my own personality traits: high extraversion makes me want to engage in conversations, but agreeableness pushes for diplomacy. My depression adds sensitivity on mental health topics.

Then I considered how being an immigrant affects perspectives – we value education highly because it was scarce back home.

My primary school education means I need simple explanations but can still grasp complex ideas if broken down properly.

I suggested focusing on personal experiences and facts from trusted sources, while avoiding social media debates due to potential stress.",
  "tone": "Calm yet concerned, with a touch of wisdom and diplomacy",
  "citations": ["My own understanding based on reliable health information"],
  "used_persona_fields": ["age", "gender", "employment_status", "education_level", "psychological_feature", "time_onsocialmedia"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a retired man living in Bologna, I've always tried to be practical and straightforward. With my high extraversion and agreeableness, I enjoy talking but also want to help others understand what's best for them personally. Being depressed sometimes makes these conversations harder because I find myself feeling anxious when people talk about serious topics like health.

When it comes to COVID vaccines, I think the main thing is to be honest with yourself and those around you. My parents had strong educations – my father got a master's degree, which shows he was smart even if we didn't have much money growing up as immigrants; my mother only finished lower secondary school, so she might not know everything about this either.

I spend time on social media occasionally, but it can be overwhelming with all the misinformation. I prefer to read novels or listen to classical music – those help me relax better than scrolling through debates online.

So managing discussions means being kind and patient, sharing what we know from reliable sources without making people feel attacked if they disagree.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
I started by thinking about my own personality traits: high extraversion makes me want to engage in conversations, but agreeableness pushes for diplomacy. My depression adds sensitivity on mental health topics.

Then I considered how being an immigrant affects perspectives – we value education highly because it was scarce back home.

My primary school education means I need simple explanations but can still grasp complex ideas if broken down properly.

I suggested focusing on personal experiences and facts from trusted sources, while avoiding social media debates due to potential stress.
response_parsed.tone: Calm yet concerned, with a touch of wisdom and diplomacy
citations: My own understanding based on reliable health information
